query: What could stomach ache mean?

plan.reason: The user is asking for general information about the meaning of stomach ache, which does not require patient-specific data.
Provide the general information about stomach ache to the user.

final_answer:
A stomach ache can have many possible causes, ranging from mild to more serious. Common causes include indigestion, gas, constipation, stomach virus, food poisoning, or overeating. It can also be related to conditions such as gastritis, ulcers, acid reflux, or in some cases, appendicitis or other abdominal issues.

If the stomach ache is severe, persistent, or accompanied by other symptoms like fever, vomiting, severe pain, or bleeding, it is important to seek medical evaluation promptly to determine the cause and appropriate treatment.

query: “I have knee pain, what should I do?”

plan.reason: Fetch trusted general information relevant to the symptom guidance request.
Provide general advice for knee pain as no patient context is available.
Conclude the response with the general advice.

medical_info.summary: Your knee joint is made up of bone, cartilage, ligaments and fluid. Muscles and tendons help the knee joint move. When any of these structures is hurt or diseased, you have knee problems. Knee problems can cause pain and difficulty walking. Knee problems are very common, and they occur in people of all ages. Knee problems can interfere with many things, from participation in sports to simply getting up from a chair and walking. This can have a big impact on your life. The most common disease affecting the knee is osteoarthritis. The cartilage in the knee gradually wears away, causing pain and swelling. Injuries to ligaments and tendons also cause knee problems. A common injury is to the anterior cruciate ligament (ACL). You usually injure your ACL by a sudden twisting motion. ACL and other knee injuries are common sports injuries. Treatment of knee problems depends on the cause. In some cases your doctor may recommend knee replacement. NIH: National Institute of Arthritis and Musculoskeletal and Skin Diseases
medical_info.disclaimer: This assistant supports administrative and educational workflows only. It does not provide a medical diagnosis or replace a licensed clinician.

final_answer:
Medical information: Knee Injuries and Disorders
Your knee joint is made up of bone, cartilage, ligaments and fluid. Muscles and tendons help the knee joint move. When any of these structures is hurt or diseased, you have knee problems. Knee problems can cause pain and difficulty walking. Knee problems are very common, and they occur in people of all ages. Knee problems can interfere with many things, from participation in sports to simply getting up from a chair and walking. This can have a big impact on your life. The most common disease affecting the knee is osteoarthritis. The cartilage in the knee gradually wears away, causing pain and swelling. Injuries to ligaments and tendons also cause knee problems. A common injury is to the anterior cruciate ligament (ACL). You usually injure your ACL by a sudden twisting motion. ACL and other knee injuries are common sports injuries. Treatment of knee problems depends on the cause. In some cases your doctor may recommend knee replacement. NIH: National Institute of Arthritis and Musculoskeletal and Skin Diseases
Sources:
- https://medlineplus.gov/

final_answer:
[Anjali Mehra] For managing stomach ache symptoms until you see Dr. Nisha Kapoor, the Gastroenterologist, you can try the following general advice:

- Avoid heavy, spicy, or greasy foods.
- Eat small, frequent meals instead of large ones.
- Stay hydrated with water or clear fluids.
- Avoid alcohol and caffeine.
- Rest and avoid strenuous activity.
- Use a warm compress on the abdomen for comfort.
- Over-the-counter antacids may help if you suspect acidity.

If symptoms worsen or you experience severe pain, vomiting, fever, or blood in stools, seek medical attention promptly.
